replication_study.hypothesis: Participants’ perceived realistic COVID-19 threat at Time 1 (mean of covid_real1–covid_real5) positively predicts their Time 2 negative affect (sum of PANAS negative items negative1–negative10) approximately one week later, controlling for symbolic COVID-19 threat at Time 1 (mean of covid_symbolic1–covid_symbolic5).

data_plan.dataset_identifier: Kachanoff_Survey_deidentify.csv (replication sample, two-wave longitudinal; provided in replication_data)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Two sessions per participant separated by approximately one week; after filtering test sessions using created timestamps (cutoff 2021-01-18 15:39:38) and attention checks.
data_plan.sample_size: To be determined upon execution; expected around N≈243 participants with two waves based on R analysis notes in Analysis_updated.Rmd.
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Individual participant (wide format: T1 predictors and T2 outcomes merged by participant_id).
data_plan.access_details: De-identified CSV bundled in the provided replication_data folder; no additional access restrictions.

qualification.explanation: This dataset contains the same constructs and longitudinal design as the original focal test: T1 perceived COVID-19 threats (realistic and symbolic) and T2 outcomes (including negative affect) measured about a week later. It provides an independent sample collected under similar conditions, enabling a robust test of the focal claim.
qualification.similarity_to_original: The original used a one-week longitudinal design (Study 3) assessing perceived realistic and symbolic threats and subsequent distress outcomes (post_registration.json; original_paper.pdf long-summary sections on Study 3). The replication dataset contains the same constructs: covid_real1–5, covid_symbolic1–5, PANAS negative items negative1–negative10, and timestamps to form T1/T2 (replication_data CSV inspected via get_dataset_info; Analysis_updated.Rmd shows same composites and SEM paths).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original studies recruited on MTurk/online panels (post_registration.json: 'Online surveys of American participants (MTurk for Study 1; online samples for Studies 2 and 3)') and demonstrated the T1–T2 effect. The replication data here are from a separate collection on Prolific with de-identified participant_id and session-based timestamps (Analysis_updated.Rmd; Kachanoff_Survey_deidentify.csv), producing a new independent sample. The updated replication code enforces attention checks and a specific time cutoff (2021-01-18 15:39:38) not explicitly described in the original, making the sample operationally distinct (Analysis_updated.Rmd).

data_plan.notes: Variables present: covid_real1–5, covid_symbolic1–5, PANAS positive1–10 and negative1–10, created timestamps, participant_id, and other scales; all required for computing T1 threat composites and T2 negative affect. Attention checks and time-based filtering are required to remove test/duplicate sessions as per Analysis_updated.Rmd. The original R code used SEM across multiple T2 outcomes; the Python translation estimates the focal path via OLS for Negative.y, consistent with the observed-variable path in SEM when modeled separately. All IO adjusted to /app/data.

steps: Filter sessions to exclude test entries using the created timestamp cutoff (2021-01-18 15:39:38) and specific duplicate sessions (as listed in Analysis_updated.Rmd).
Compute attention_total from checks (attention_check1–5, with regex scoring for attention_check5) and retain participants with attention_total >= 4.

Score composites at each wave: Realistic = mean(covid_real1–5), Symbolic = mean(covid_symbolic1–5), Negative = sum of PANAS negatives (negative1–negative10). Recode BAI and IES items as in the R script. Apply 80% completion rule for Intrusion/Avoidance sums.
Order rows by created (ascending) per participant; create wide-format dataset by merging the first occurrence (T1, suffix .x) with the second occurrence (T2, suffix .y) using participant_id.
Estimate the focal model: OLS Negative.y ~ Realistic.x + Symbolic.x using listwise deletion on model variables.
planned_method.models: Ordinary Least Squares regression approximating the observed-variable SEM path coefficient for T2 negative affect on T1 threats.
planned_method.outcome_variable: Negative.y (sum of PANAS negative items at T2).
planned_method.independent_variables: Realistic.x (mean of covid_real1–5 at T1); Symbolic.x (mean of covid_symbolic1–5 at T1).
planned_method.control_variables: None in the focal test; optional robustness could include baseline Negative.x.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Coefficient on Realistic.x in OLS predicting Negative.y, controlling for Symbolic.x.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: t-test on the Realistic.x coefficient (two-tailed, alpha=0.05).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Composite scores computed with NA-robust functions (na.rm), with 80% item completion rule for Intrusion/Avoidance; listwise deletion for model estimation.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Not applicable for the single focal outcome; no correction planned.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Statistical significance at p < 0.05 (two-tailed) with a positive direction expected for Realistic.x.

files.replication_data/Analysis_updated__py.py: Python translation of Analysis_updated.Rmd. Reads /app/data/Kachanoff_Survey_deidentify.csv, applies time and attention filters, computes composites, merges T1/T2 by participant_id, and runs OLS for Negative.y ~ Realistic.x + Symbolic.x. Saves /app/data/DFwide.csv and /app/data/replication_results.json.
files.replication_data/README_run.txt: Instructions to execute the Python analysis and lists the outputs generated in /app/data.

codebase.notes: All IO is directed to /app/data to comply with execution environment requirements. The Python script approximates the SEM focal path using OLS for observed variables, which matches the path coefficient interpretation for the specific link of interest. If SEM is required later, packages like semopy could be added, but current analysis targets the focal path directly.

instructions: Place Kachanoff_Survey_deidentify.csv into ./data so it is available at /app/data inside the container.
Inspect outputs: /app/data/DFwide.csv and /app/data/replication_results.json for coefficient and p-value on Realistic.x.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare the sign and significance of the Realistic.x coefficient on Negative.y to the original (expected positive; original report indicates b≈4.02, p=0.000 per initial_details.txt). Also report effect size magnitude differences and 95% CI if needed.
